title: Civil Aviation: Subsidiary Legislation: Civil Aviation (construction of Visual and Instrument Flight Procedures) Regulations
title_en: Civil Aviation: Subsidiary Legislation: Civil Aviation (construction of Visual and Instrument Flight Procedures) Regulations

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content:
1. Citation
These Regulations may be cited as the Civil Aviation (Construction of Visual and Instrument Flight Procedures) Regulations.

provisions.title: Interpretation
Application
provisions.content:
3. Application
These Regulations shall apply to any person providing an instrument flight procedure design service within a designated airspace and at an aerodrome for civil aviation purposes.

provisions.title: Requirements for provision of instrument flight procedure design service
provisions.content:
4. Requirements for provision of instrument flight procedure design service
(1) An air navigation service provider shall—
( a ) provide an instrument flight procedure design service;
( b ) agree with one or more air navigation service providers to provide a joint instrument flight procedure design service; or
( c ) delegate the provision of the instrument flight procedure design service to an external agency.
(2) A person shall not provide an instrument flight procedure design service within Botswana unless—
( a ) he or she holds a certificate issued under the Civil Aviation (Certification of Air Navigation Service Providers) Regulations (Cap. 71:01 (Sub. Leg.)); and

definitions.definition:
the limits of usability of an aerodrome for—
( a ) take-off, expressed in terms of runway visual range or visibility and, if necessary, cloud conditions
a representation of aeronautical facts, concepts or instructions in a formalised manner suitable for communication, interpretation or processing
information resulting from the assembly, analysis and formatting of aeronautical data

a notice containing information that does not qualify for the origination of a NOTAM or for inclusion in the AIP, but which relates to flight safety, air navigation, technical, administrative or legislative matters
a publication issued by or with the authority of a State and containing aeronautical information of a lasting character essential to air navigation

a service established within the defined area of coverage responsible for the provision of aeronautical data and aeronautical information necessary for the safety, regularity and efficiency of air navigation

the Aeronautical Information Regulation and Control signifying a system aimed at advance notification, based on common effective dates, of circumstances that necessitate significant changes in operating practices
an independent entity established for the purpose of providing one or more of the air navigation services

one or more of the following services provided for air navigation—
( a ) air traffic services or air traffic management
a specified route designed for channelling the flow of traffic as necessary for the provision of air traffic services
the certificate for the provision of Air Navigation Services issued by the Authority under Part II of these Regulations
a person responsible for flight procedure design who meets the competency requirements as laid down by the Authority
a service established for the design, documentation, validation, maintenance and periodic review of instrument flight procedures necessary for the safety, regularity and efficiency of air navigation

all fixed objects, whether temporary or permanent, or mobile objects, or parts thereof, that—
( a ) are located on an area intended for the surface movement of aircraft
a person, organisation or enterprise engaged in or offering to engage in an aircraft operation
a manual prepared by a service provider or a person applying for approval
an approach procedure designed for helicopters only that includes both a visual and an instrument segment
a degree to which a set of inherent characteristics fulfils requirement
part of quality management focused on providing confidence that quality requirements will be fulfilled
co-ordinated activities to direct and control an organisation with regard to quality
the surface of the earth containing naturally occurring features such as mountains, hills, ridges, valleys, bodies of water, permanent ice and snow, and excluding obstacles.
